rationale: The citing paper’s core outcomes include consumers choosing more feature-rich products and addresses the customization paradox, while the cited paper is directly about preference for feature-rich vs. feature-poor options and feature-fatigue mechanisms (social utility/usability trade-offs). Given this strong construct overlap, the citation could plausibly serve as a substantive theory/background source rather than a generic mention, even though only DOI-level match evidence is available.
